reference: Ceanglaíonn an bille aistreog leis an uimhir "18" a bheith mar lipéad ar fhíschluichí a dhíoltar i stát California, agus soláthraítear ann gur féidir fíneáil $1000 in aghaidh an chiona a ghearradh as iad a dhíol le mionaoiseach.

source: The Director of Public Prosecutions, Kier Starmer QC, gave a statement this morning announcing the prosecution of both Huhne and Pryce.
reference: Thug Kier Starmer QC, Stiúrthóir na Ionchúiseamh Poiblí, ráiteas ar maidin ag fógairt go n-ionchúiseofar Huhne agus Pryce araon.

source: Huhne has resigned and he will be replaced in the Cabinet by Ed Davey MP. Norman Lamb MP is expected to take the Business Minister job Davey is vacating.
reference: Tá Huhne tar éis éirí as agus beidh Ed Davey MP ag dul ina áit sa Chomh-Aireacht. Meastar go nglacfaidh Norman Lamb MP post an Aire Gnó a d'fhág Davey.

source: Huhne and Pryce are scheduled to appear at the Westminster Magistrates Court on February 16.
reference: Tá Huhne agus Pryce beartaithe le bheith i láthair i gCúirt Ghiúistís Westminster an 16 Feabhra.

source: The fatalities were Nicholas Alden, 25, and Zachary Cuddeback, 21. Cuddeback had been the driver.
reference: Ba iad Nicholas Alden, 25, agus Zachary Cuddeback, 21 an bheirt a maraíodh. Cuddeback a bhí ag tiomáint.

source: Edgar Veguilla received arm and jaw wounds while Kristoffer Schneider was left requiring reconstructive surgery for his face.
reference: Gortaíodh lámh agus giall de chuid Edgar Veguilla agus fágadh Kristoffer Schneider sa chaoi go bhfuil obráid athchruthaitheach de dhíth ar a aghaidh.

source: Uka's weapon failed whilst pointed at a fifth man's head. Schneider has ongoing pain, blindness in one eye, a missing section of skull and a face rebuilt from titanium.
reference: Theip ar arm Uka fad a bhí sé dírithe ar cheann an chúigiú fhir. Tá pian ag Schnieder mar aon le daille i súil amháin, tá cuid dá chloigeann ar iarraidh agus rinneadh a aghaidh a atógáil as tíotáiniam.

source: Schneider testified via videolink from a USAF base in his homeland.
reference: Thug Schneider a fhianaise trí fhíschomhdháil ó bhunáit USAF ina thír dhúchais.

source: Beyond Wednesday's event, Carpanedo competed in two individual races at the Championships.
reference: Taobh amuigh d'imeacht Dé Céadaoin, bhí Carpanedo páirteach in dhá rás aonair ag na Craobhchomórtais.

source: Her first was the Slalom, where she earned a Did Not Finish in her first run. 36 of the 116 competitors had the same result in that race.
reference: Ba é an Slalóm a céad rás, ina bhfuair sí 'Gan Chríochnú' dá céad fána. Bhí an toradh céanna ag 36 as na 116 iomaitheoir sa rás sin.

source: Her other race, the Giant Slalom, saw her finish in tenth in the women's sitting group with a combined run time of 4:41.30, 2:11.60 minutes slower than first place finisher Austrian Claudia Loesch and 1:09.02 minutes slower than the ninth place finisher Gyöngyi Dani of Hungary.
reference: Sa dara rás aici, an Mórshlalóm, tháinig sí sa deichiú háit i ngrúpa suí na mban le ham iomlán fánaí de 4:41.30, 2:11.60 nóiméad níos moille ná an buaiteoir, Claudia Loesch ón Ostair agus 1:09.02 níos moille ná Gyöngyi Dani ón Ungáir a chríochnaigh sa naoú háit.

source: Four skiers in the women's sitting group failed to finish their runs, and 45 of the 117 total skiers in the Giant Slalom failed to rank in the race.
reference: Theip ar cheathrar sciálaithe i ngrúpa suí na mban a gcuid fánaí a chríochnú, agus theip ar 45 as na 117 sciálaithe sa Mhórshlalóm ar an iomlán, rangú a fháil sa rás.

source: The Madhya Pradesh Police recovered the stolen laptop and mobile phone.
reference: Fuair Póilíní Madhya Pradesh an ríomhaire glúine agus an fón póca goidte ar ais.

source: Deputy Inspector General D K Arya said, "We have arrested five persons who raped the Swiss woman and recovered her mobile and laptop".
reference: Dúirt an Leas-Ardchigire D K Arya, "Tá cúigear gafa againn a rinne éigniú ar an mbean Eilvéiseach, agus fuaireamar ar ais a fón móibíleach agus a ríomhaire glúine."

source: The accused are named as Baba Kanjar, Bhutha Kanjar, Rampro Kanjar, Gaza Kanjar and Vishnu Kanjar.
reference: Baba Kanjar, Bhutha Kanjar, Rampro Kanjar, Gaza Kanjar agus Vishnu Kanjar is ainm dóibh siúd atá cúisithe.

source: Police superintendent Chandra Shekhar Solanki said the accused appeared in court with covered faces.
reference: Dúirt Chandra Shekhar Solanki, ceannfort na bpóilíní, gur tháinig na cúisithe isteach sa chúirt agus clúdach ar a n-aghaidheanna.

source: Although three people were inside the house when the car impacted it, none of them were hurt.
reference: Ainneoin go raibh triúr sa teach nuair a bhuail an carr é, níor gortaíodh duine ar bith acu.

source: However, the driver sustained serious injuries to the head.
reference: Mar sin féin, bhain gortuithe tromchúiseacha dá chloigeann.

source: The road where the crash happened was temporarily closed while emergency services freed the driver from the red Audi TT.
reference: Dúnadh an bóthar mar ar tharla an tuairteáil ar feadh tamaill fad a bhí na seirbhísí éigeandála ag scaoileadh an tiománaí as Audi TT dearg.

source: He was initially hospitalised in the James Paget Hospital in Great Yarmouth.
reference: In Ospidéal James Paget i Yarmouth Mór a bhí sé i dtosach.

source: He was subsequently relocated to Addenbrooke's Hospital in Cambridge.
reference: Ina dhiaidh sin, bogadh chuig Ospidéal Addenbrooke i gCambridge é.

source: Adekoya has since been in Edinburgh Sheriff Court charged with murdering her son.
reference: Is i gCúirt Sirriam Dhún Éideann atá Adekoya ó shin agus dúnmharú a mic féin curtha ina leith.

source: She is in custody pending indictment and trial, but any eyewitness evidence may be tainted because her image has been widely published.
reference: Tá sí faoi choimeád ar feitheamh díotála agus trialach, ach is féidir go mbeidh aon fhianaise ó fhinnéithe súl truaillithe toisc gur foilsíodh pictiúir di go forleathan.

source: This is common practice elsewhere in the UK but Scottish justice works differently and courts have viewed publication of photos as potentially prejudicial.
reference: Is gnáthchleachtas é seo in áiteanna eile sa RA ach oibríonn ceartas na hAlban ar bhealach difriúil agus mheas cúirteanna go bhféadfadh foilsiú grianghraf a bheith dochrach.

source: Professor Pamela Ferguson of the University of Dundee notes "journalists do seem to be walking a dangerous line if publishing photos etc of suspects."
reference: Dúirt an tOllamh Pamela Ferguson ó Ollscoil Dundee "is cosúil nach bhfuil an chothromaíocht cheart á coimeád ag iriseoirí má fhoilsíonn siad pictiúir srl. d'amhrastaigh."

source: Crown Office, which is in overall charge of prosecutions, has indicated to journalists that no further comment will be made at least until indictment.
reference: Chuir Oifig an Choróin, atá i gceannas go foriomlán ar ionchúisimh, in iúl d'iriseoirí nach ndéanfar a thuilleadh tráchta faoi go dtí go ndéanfar díotáil ar a laghad.

source: The document, according to the leak, will refer to the borders dispute, which Palestine wants based on the borders before the 1967 Mideast War.
reference: Déanfaidh an doiciméad, de réir an sceite, tagairt don aighneas faoi theorainneacha, teorainneacha atá á lorg ag an bPalaistín bunaithe ar na teorainneacha a bhí ann roimh Chogadh an Mheánoirthir i 1967.

source: Other topics covered reportedly include the future state of Jerusalem which is sacred to both nations and the Jordan Valley issue.
reference: Deirtear go raibh todhchaí stáit Iarúsailéim, atá naofa don dá thír, agus ceist Ghleann na hIordáine i measc na n-ábhar eile a clúdaíodh.

source: Israel demands an ongoing military presence in the valley for ten years once an agreement is signed while the PA agrees to leave such presence only for five years.
reference: Éilíonn Iosrael go mbeadh láithreacht mhíleata leanúnach sa ghleann ar feadh deich mbliana nuair a shínítear comhaontú ach ní aontaíonn an PA a bheith i láthair ach ar feadh cúig bliana.

source: Shooters in the supplementary pest control trial were to be closely supervised by rangers, as the trial was monitored and its effectiveness evaluated.
reference: Bhí maoir le maoirsiú dlúth a dhéanamh ar lámhachóirí sa triail fhorlíontach um rialú lotnaidí, de réir mar a dhéanfaí monatóireacht ar an triail agus meastóireacht ar a éifeachtaí a bhí sé.

source: In a partnership of NPWS and the Sporting Shooters Association of Australia (NSW) Inc, qualified volunteers were recruited, under the Sporting Shooters Association's hunting program.
reference: I gcomhpháirtíocht le NPWS agus Cumann Lámhachóirí Spórt na hAstráile (NSW) Inc, earcaíodh saorálaithe cáilithe, faoi chlár seilge den Chumann Lámhachóirí Spórt.

source: According to Mick O'Flynn, the Acting Director Park Conservation and Heritage with the NPWS, the four shooters selected for the first shooting operation received comprehensive safety and training instruction.
reference: Deir Mick O'Flynn, Stiúrthóir Gníomhach um Chaomhnú agus Oidhreacht na bPáirceanna leis an tSeirbhís Náisiúnta Páirceanna agus Fiadhúlra, gur cuireadh oiliúint chuimsitheach sábháilteachta agus traenála ar an gceathrar lámhachóirí a roghnaíodh don chéad bhabhta lámhaigh.

source: Martelly swore in a new Provisional Electoral Council (CEP) of nine members yesterday.
reference: Chuir Martely Comhairle Toghcháin Shealadach (CEP) nua de naonúr faoi mhionn inné.

source: It is Martelly's fifth CEP in four years.
reference: Is é seo an cúigiú CEP a bhí ag Martelly le ceithre bliana anuas.

source: Last month a presidential commission recommended the prior CEP's resignation as part of a package of measures to move the country towards new elections.
reference: An mhí seo caite mhol coimisiún uachtaránachta go n-éireodh an CEP roimhe as mar chuid de phacáiste beart leis an tír a bhogadh i dtreo toghchán úra.

source: The commission was Martelly's response to widespread anti-regime protests that started in October.
reference: Ba é an Coimisiún freagra Martelly ar agóidí forleathana in aghaidh an réimis, agóidí a thosaigh i mí Dheireadh Fómhair.

source: The sometimes-violent protests were triggered by failure to hold elections, some due since 2011.
reference: Ba í an fhaillí i dtoghcháin a reáchtáil, cuid acu a bhí dlite ó 2011, a ba chúis leis na hagóidí, a bhí foréigneach ar uairibh.

source: Around 60 cases of malfunctioning iPods overheating have been reported, causing a total of six fires and leaving four people with minor burns.
reference: Tá thart ar 60 cás tuairiscithe inar éirí iPod mífheidhmiúil róthe, ar an iomlán tharla sé dóiteán dá bharr, agus fágadh ceathrar a raibh mionghortuithe dó acu.

source: Japan's Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry (METI) said that it had been aware of 27 accidents related to the devices.
reference: Deir Aireacht Geilleagair, Trádála agus Tráchtála na Seapáine (METI) go raibh siad ar an eolas faoi 27 timpiste bainteach leis na gléasanna.

source: Last week, METI announced that Apple had informed it of 34 additional overheating incidents, which the company called "non-serious."
reference: An tseachtain seo caite, d'fhógair METI gur chuir Apple iad ar an eolas faoi 34 eachtra róthéimh bhreise, ar a ghlaoigh an comhlacht "neamh-thromchúiseach."

source: The ministry responded by calling Apple's postponement of the report "truly regrettable."
reference: Ba é freagra na haireachta a rá gur "cúis aiféala amach is amach" a bhí ann gur chuir Apple an tuarascáil ar atráth.

source: The eathquake struck Mariana at 07:19 a.m. local time (09:19 p.m. GMT Friday).
reference: Ag naoi nóiméad déag tar éis a seacht (09:19 p.m. GMT Dé hAoine) ar maidin a bhuail an crith talún Mariana.

source: The Northern Marianas emergency management office said that there were no damages reported in the nation.
reference: Dúirt oifig bainistíochta éigeandála Thuaisceart Marianas nár tuairiscíodh aon damáiste sa náisiún.

source: Also the Pacific Tsunami Warning Center said that there was no Tsunami indication.
reference: Thug Ionad Rabhaidh Súnámaí an Aigéin Chiúin le fios freisin nach raibh aon chomhartha Súnámaí ann.

source: A former Filipino policeman has kept Hong Kong tourists hostage by hijacking their bus in Manila, the capital of the Philippines.
reference: Rinne iar-phóilín Filipíneach giall de thurasóirí Hong Cong nuair a d'fhuadaigh sé a mbus i Mainile, príomhchathair na nOileán Filipíneach.

source: Rolando Mendoza fired his M16 rifle at the tourists.
reference: Scaoil Rolanda Mendoza a raidhfil M16 leis na turasóirí.

source: Several hostages have been rescued and least six have been confirmed dead so far.
reference: Tarrtháladh roinnt giall agus deimhníodh gur cailleadh seisear ar a laghad go dtí seo.

source: Six hostages, including the children and elderly, were released early, as were the Filipino photographers.
reference: Scaoileadh sé ghiall, lena n-áirítear na leanaí agus na daoine scothaosta, go luath, mar aon leis na grianghrafadóirí Filipíneacha.

source: The photographers later took the place of an aged lady as she needed the lavatory. Mendoza was gunned down.
reference: Níos déanaí ghlac na grianghrafadóirí áit seanmhná de bhrí gur theastaigh uaithi an leithreas a úsáid. Leagadh Mendoza le piléar.

source: Liggins followed in his father’s footsteps and entered a career in medicine.
reference: Rinne Liggins mar a rinne a athair roimhe agus chuaigh sé le gairm bheatha sa leigheas.

source: He trained as an obstetrician and began to work at the Auckland's National Women's Hospital in 1959.
reference: Fuair sé oiliúint mar obstatraí agus thosaigh sé ag obair in Ospidéal Náisiúnta na mBan Auckland i 1959.

source: While he was working at the hospital Liggins began to investigate premature labor during his spare time.
reference: Fad a bhí sé ag obair san ospidéal thosaigh Liggins ag déanamh imscrúdú, ina chuid ama saoir, ar luí seoil roimh am.

source: His research showed that if a hormone was administered it would speed up the baby's foetal lung maturation.
reference: Léirigh a chuid taighde go n-éireoidh aibiú ar scamhóga féatais níos tapúla le hormón a thabhairt.

source: Xinhua reported that government investigators recovered two 'black box' flight recorders on Wednesday.
reference: Chualathas ó Xinhua gur tháinig imscrúdaitheoirí rialtais ar dhá cheann de thaifeadán eitilte 'bosca dubh' Dé Céadaoin.

source: Fellow wrestlers also paid tribute to Luna.
reference: Thug comh-iomrascálaithe a n-ómós do Luna freisin.

source: Tommy Dreamer said "Luna was the first Queen of Extreme. My first manager. Luna passed away on the night of two moons. Pretty unique just like her. Strong woman."
reference: Dúirt Tommy Dreamer "Ba í Luna an chéad Queen of Extreme. An chéad bhainisteoir a bhí agam. Cailleadh Luna ar oíche an dá ghealaí. Bhí sé sin chomh neamhghnách léi féin. Bean láidir."

source: Dustin "Goldust" Runnels commented that "Luna was as freaky as me...maybe even more...love her and will miss her...hopefully she's in a better place."
reference: De réir trácht a rinne Dustin "Goldust" Runnels "Bhí Luna chomh saoithiúil liom féin...níos mó ná sin b'fhéidir...tá grá agam uirthi agus aireoidh sí go mór uaim...tá súil agam go bhfuil sí in áit níos fearr anois."

source: Out of 1,400 people polled prior to the 2010 federal election, those who oppose Australia becoming a republic grew by 8 per cent since 2008.
reference: As 1,400 duine a ndearnadh pobalbhreith orthu roimh thoghchán cónaidhme 2010, tháinig méadú 8 faoin gcéad ó 2008 i leith ar líon na ndaoine a chuireann i gcoinne na hAstráile a bheith ina poblacht.

source: Caretaker Prime Minister Julia Gillard claimed during the campaign of the 2010 federal election that she believed Australia should become a republic at the end of Queen Elizabeth II's reign.
reference: Mhaígh an Príomh-Aire Sealadach Julia Gillard i rith fheachtas an toghcháin feidearálaigh in 2010 gur chreid sí gur chóir don Astráil a bheith ina poblacht ag deireadh réim Bhanríon Éilis II.

source: 34 per cent of those in the poll share this view, wanting Queen Elizabeth II to be Australia's last monarch.
reference: Tá an dearcadh céanna ag 34 faoin gcéad den phobalbhreith, ar mian leo go mbeidh an Bhanríon Éilis II ina monarc deiridh i réim san Astráil.

source: At the extremes of the poll, 29 per cent of those surveyed believe Australia should become a republic as soon as possible, while 31 per cent believe Australia should never become a republic.
reference: Ag pointí foircneacha den phobalbhreith, creideann 29 faoin gcéad as siúd a ceistíodh sa suirbhé go mba cheart don Astráil teacht ina poblacht chomh luath agus is féidir, agus creideann 31 faoin gcéad nár chóir don Astráil a bheith ina poblacht riamh.

source: The Olympic gold medalist was due to swim in the 100m and 200m freestyle and in three relays at the Commonwealth Games, but due to his complaints his fitness has been in doubt.
reference: Bhí buaiteoir an bhoinn óir Oilimpigh le snámh sa chomórtas saorstíle 100m agus 200m agus i dtrí rás sealaíochta Cluichí an Chomhlathais, ach mar gheall ar a ghearáin bhí a chorpacmhainneacht faoi amhras.

source: He has been unable to take the drugs needed to overcome his pain as they are banned from the Games.
reference: Ní raibh sé in ann na drugaí, a bhfuil ag teastáil lena phian a mhaolú, a ghlacadh toisc go bhfuil siad coiscthe ag na Cluichí.

source: Curtis Cooper, a mathematician and computer science professor at the University of Central Missouri, has discovered the largest known prime number to date on January 25.
reference: Is é Curtis Cooper, matamaiticeoir agus ollamh le ríomheolaíocht in Ollscoil Missouri Láir, a fuair amach an uimhir phríomha is mó a bhfuil eolas uirthi go dtí an dáta seo 25ú Eanáir.

source: Several people verified the discovery using different hardware and software by the beginning of February and it was announced on Tuesday.
reference: Bhí an fhionnachtain fíoraithe ag roinnt daoine faoi thús mhí Feabhra, trí chrua-earraí agus bogearraí éagsúla a úsáid, agus fógraíodh é Dé Máirt.

source: Comets may possibly have been a source of water delivery to the earth along with organic matter that can form proteins and support life.
reference: Tá an fhéidearthacht ann gurbh fhoinsí iad na cóiméid de sheachadadh uisce chuig an domhan in éineacht le hábhar orgánach a bhí in ann próitéine a dhéanamh agus an bheatha a chothú.

source: Scientists hope to understand how planets form, especially how the Earth formed, since comets collided with the Earth long ago.
reference: Tá súil ag eolaithe tuiscint a fháil ar an gcaoi a dtagann ann do phláinéid, go háirithe cén chaoi ar tháinig ann don Domhan, ó bhuail cóiméid faoin Domhan fadó.

source: Cuomo, 53, began his governorship earlier this year and signed a bill last month legalizing same-sex marriage.
reference: Chuir Cuomo, 53, tús lena ghobharnóireacht níos luaithe i mbliana agus shínigh sé bille an mhí seo caite lena ndéantar dleathach an pósadh comhghnéis.

source: He referred to the rumors as "political chatter and silliness".
reference: Dúirt sé nach raibh ann sna ráflaí ach "clabaireacht pholaitiúil agus seafóid".

source: He is speculated to make a run for president in 2016.
reference: Tá tuairimíocht ann go seasfaidh sé i dtoghchán na huachtaránachta in 2016.

source: NextGen is a system the FAA claims would allow aircraft to fly shorter routes and save millions of gallons of fuel each year and cut carbon emissions.
reference: Is córas é NextGen a mhaíonn an FAA a ligfeadh d’aerárthaí bealaí níos giorra a eitilt agus na milliúin galún breosla a shábháil gach bliain agus astaíochtaí carbóin a ghearradh.

source: It uses satellite-based technology as opposed to older ground-radar-based technology to allow air traffic controllers to pinpoint aircraft with greater precision and give pilots more accurate information.
reference: Baineann sé leas as teicneolaíocht shatailítbhunaithe seachas an seanteicneolaíocht radar-talún-bhunaithe le ligeant do rialtóir aerthráchta aerárthaigh a aimsiú ar bhealach níos cruinne agus eolas níos cruinne a thabhairt do phíolótaí.

source: No extra transport is being put on and overground trains will not stop at Wembley, and car parking and park-and-ride facilities are unavailable at the ground.
reference: Níl aon iompar breise á chur ar fáil agus ní stadfaidh traenacha os cionn talún ag Wembley, agus níl saoráidí carrpháirceála ná páirceála agus taistil ar fáil ag an bpáirc.

source: Fears of lack of transportation raised the possibility that the game would be forced to play behind closed doors without the team's supporters.
reference: Bhí imní ann faoi easpa iompair agus an seans dá réir go mbeadh sé éigeantach an cluiche a imirt ar chúl doirse druidte agus gan lucht leanúna na foirne.

source: A study published on Thursday in the journal Science reported on formation of a new bird species on the Ecuadorean Galápagos Islands.
reference: I staidéar a foilsíodh Déardaoin san iris Science, tuairiscíodh faoi chruthú speiceas nua éin ar Oileáin Galápagos de chuid an Eacuadór.

source: Researchers from Princeton University in the United States and Uppsala University in Sweden reported the new species evolved in just two generations, though this process had been believed to take much longer, due to breeding between an endemic Darwin finch, Geospiza fortes, and the immigrant cactus finch, Geospiza conirostris.
reference: Thuairiscigh taighdeoirí ó Ollscoil Princeton sna Stáit Aontaithe agus Ollscoil Uppsala sa tSualainn gur tháinig an speiceas nua chun cinn laistigh de dhá ghlúin, cé gur measadh gur thóg an próiseas seo i bhfad níos faide, mar gheall ar phórú idir glasán Darwin dúchasach, Geospiza forteas, agus an glasán cachtais inimirceach, Geospiza conirostris.

source: Gold may be worked into all sorts of shapes. It can be rolled into tiny shapes.
reference: Is féidir ór a shaoirsiú in a lán cineál de chruthanna. Is féidir é a rolladh ina gcruthanna bídeacha.

source: It can be pulled into thin wire, which can be twisted and plaited. It can be hammered or rolled into sheets.
reference: Is féidir é a tharraingt go dtí go bhfuil sé ina sreang tanaí, ar féidir é a thumadh agus a phlátáil. Is féidir é a bhualadh le casúr nó a rolladh amach i leatháin.

source: It can be made very thin, and stuck onto other metal. It can be made so thin that it was sometimes used to decorate the hand-painted pictures in books called "illuminated manuscripts".
reference: Is féidir é a dhéanamh an-tanaí, agus a bheith greamaithe de mhiotal eile. Is féidir é a dhéanamh chomh tanaí gur úsáideadh é uaireanta chun na pictiúir lámhphéinteáilte a mhaisiú i leabhair ar a dtugtar "script dhathmhaisithe".

source: This is called a chemical's pH. You can make an indicator using red cabbage juice.
reference: pH an cheimiceáin a thugtar air seo. Is féidir táscaire a dhéanamh as sú cabáiste deirge.

source: The cabbage juice changes color depending on how acidic or basic (alkaline) the chemical is.
reference: Athraíonn dath an tsú chabáiste ag brath ar a aigéadaí nó a bhunata (a alcailí) atá an ceimiceán.

source: The pH level is indicated by the amount of Hydrogen (the H in pH) ions in the tested chemical.
reference: Líon na n-ian hidrigine (an H in pH) sa cheimiceán tástáilte is ea a léiríonn an leibhéal pH.

source: Hydrogen ions are protons that had their electrons stripped off them (since Hydrogen atoms consist of one proton and one electron).
reference: Is prótóin iad iain hidrigine ar baineadh a leictreoin díobh (ós rud é go bhfuil adamh hidrigine comhdhéanta de phrótón agus leictreon amháin).

source: Swirl the two dry powders together and then, with clean wet hands, squeeze them into a ball.
reference: Measc dhá phúdar thirime le chéile le fuinneamh agus úsáid lámha glana agus fliucha chun iad a fháisceadh ina liathróid.

source: The moisture on your hands will react with the outer layers, which will feel funny and form a sort of shell.
reference: Imoibreoidh an taise ar do lámh leis na sraitheanna is faide amach, a mhothóidh aisteach agus a dhéanfaidh cineál sliogáin.

source: The cities of Harappa and Mohenjo-daro had a flush toilet in almost every house, attached to a sophisticated sewage system.
reference: Bhí leithreas sruthlaithe i ngach teach, nach mór, i gcathracha Harappa agus Mohenjo-daro, agus iad ceangailte le córas séarachais sofaisticiúil.

source: Remains of sewage systems have been found in the houses of the Minoan cities of Crete and Santorini in Greece.
reference: Táthar tar éis teacht ar iarsmaí de chórais séarachais i dtithe chathracha Mionócha na Créite agus Santorini sa Ghréig.

source: There were also toilets in ancient Egypt, Persia and China. In Roman civilization, toilets were sometimes part of public bath houses where men and women were together in mixed company.
reference: Ina theanta sin, bhí leithris san Éigipt, sa Pheirs agus sa tSín ársa. I sibhialtacht na Róimhe, bhí leithris mar chuid de thithe folctha poiblí uaireanta, mar a chruinníodh fir agus mná le chéile i gcuideachta mheasctha.

source: When you call someone who is thousands of miles away, you are using a satellite.
reference: Nuair a ghlaonn tú ar dhuine atá na mílte míle i bhfad uait, tá satailít in úsáid agat.

source: The satellite in space gets the call and then reflects it back down, almost instantly.
reference: Faigheann an satailít sa spás an glao agus frithchaitear ar ais anuas é, láithreach, nach mór.

source: The satellite was sent into space by a rocket. Scientists use telescopes in space because the Earth’s atmosphere distorts some of our light and view.
reference: Roicéad a chuir an tsatailít isteach sa spás. Úsáideann eolaithe teileascóip sa spás de bhrí go gcuireann atmaisféar an Domhain cuid dár solais agus dár radhairc as riocht.

source: It takes a giant rocket over a 100 feet high to put a satellite or telescope in space.
reference: Glacann sé roicéad ollmhór os cionn 100 troigh ar airde le satailít nó teileascóp a chur suas sa spás.

source: The wheel has changed the world in incredible ways. The biggest thing that the wheel has done for us is given us much easier and faster transportation.
reference: Is dochreidte an t-athrú ar an domhan ó rinneadh an roth. An rud is mó a tháinig as ná córais iompair atá níos fusa agus níos tapa.

source: It has brought us the train, the car, and many other transportation devices.
reference: Is as sin a tháinig an traein, an carr agus neart córacha iompair eile.

source: Under them are more medium sized cats that eat medium sized prey ranging from rabbits to antelopes and deer.
reference: Fúthu gheofar cait níos meánmhéide a itheann creach meánmhéide ó choiníní go hantalóip agus fianna.

source: Finally, there are many small cats (including loose pet cats) that eat the far more numerous small prey like insects, rodents, lizards, and birds.
reference: Faoi dheireadh, tá go leor cat beag ann (lena n-áirítear cait mar pheataí agus iad amuigh) a itheann an creach beag atá níos líonmhaire fós amhail feithidí, creimirí, laghairteanna agus éin.

source: The secret to their success is the concept of the niche, a special job each cat holds that keeps it from competing with others.
reference: Is é coincheap na nideoige an tslí is fusa leis an rath a bhíonn orthu a mhíniú, tá jab speisialta ag gach cat aonair ionas nach mbíonn sé ag dul san iomaíocht le cait eile.

source: Lions are the most social cats, living in large groups called prides.
reference: Is iad na leoin na cait is sóisialta, maireann siad i ngrúpaí móra ar a dtugtar ealtaí.

source: Prides are made up of one to three related adult males, along with as many as thirty females and cubs.
reference: Idir aosach fireann amháin agus trí cinn ghaolmhara a bhíonn in ealta leon, mar aon le suas le tríocha baineann agus coileáin.

source: The females are usually closely related to each other, being a large family of sisters and daughters.
reference: Is gnách dlúthghaol a bheith idir na baineannaigh, de bhrí gur teaghlach mór deirfiúracha agus iníonacha é.

source: Lion prides act much like packs of wolves or dogs, animals surprisingly similar to lions (but not other big cats) in behavior, and also very deadly to their prey.
reference: Déanann ealta leon iad féin a iompar mar phaca mac tíre nó gadhair, ainmhithe atá cosúil le leoin (ach ní cait mhóra eile) ina gcuid iompair aisteach go leor agus marfach go deo dá gcreach chomh maith.

source: A well rounded athlete, the tiger can climb (though not well), swim, leap great distances and pull with five times the force of a strong human.
reference: Is lúthchleasaí cuimsitheach é an tíogar, ar féidir leis dreapadh (ach ní go maith), snámh, faid mhóra a léim agus tarraingt le fórsa atá cúig huaire níos láidre ná duine láidir.

source: The tiger is in the same group (Genus Panthera) as lions, leopards, and jaguars. These four cats are the only ones who can roar.
reference: Baineann an tíogar leis an ngrúpa céanna (Géineas Panthera) agus an leon, an liopard agus an iaguar. Is iad na ceithre chait seo amháin as na cait uile ar féidir leo búir a dhéanamh.

source: The tiger's roar is not like the full-voiced roar of a lion, but more like a sentence of snarly, shouted words.
reference: Níl búir an tíogair cosúil le búir lánghlórach an leoin, tá sé níos cosúla le habairt d'fhocail dhrannacha, bhéicthe.

source: Ocelots like to eat small animals. They will catch monkeys, snakes, rodents and birds if they can. Almost all of the animals that the ocelot hunts are far smaller than it is.
reference: Is maith le hosalait ainmhithe beaga a ithe. Gabhfaidh siad moncaithe, nathracha, creimirí agus éin más féidir leo. Tá beagnach gach ceann de na hainmhithe a sheilgeann an t-osalat níos lú ná é.

source: Scientists think that ocelots follow and find animals to eat (prey) by smell, sniffing for where they've been on the ground.
reference: Síleann eolaithe go n-úsáideann osalait a gcumas bolaidh chun ainmhithe a aimsiú lena n-ithe (creach), ag smúrthacht a rian ar an talamh.

source: They can see very well in the dark with night vision, and move very stealthily, too. Ocelots hunt their prey by blending in with their surroundings then pouncing on their prey.
reference: Tá radharc oíche acu a thugann radharc an-mhaith dóibh sa dorchadas, agus bogann siad go fáilí, leis. Déanann osalait a gcreach a sheilg trí bheith ar aon dath lena dtimpeallacht agus ansin léim ar a gcreach.

source: When a small group of living things (a small population) gets separated from the main population that they came from (like if they move over a mountain range or a river, or if they move to a new island so that they can't easily move back) they will often find themselves in a different environment than they were in before.
reference: Nuair a scarann ​​grúpa beag rudaí beo (daonra beag) ón bpríomh-dhaonra as ar tháinig siad (má bhogann siad thar shliabhraon nó abhainn, nó má bhogann siad go hoileán nua ionas nach mbeidh siad in ann filleadh go héasca) is minic a bhraithfidh siad go bhfuil siad i dtimpeallacht nach ionann agus an timpeallacht ina raibh siad roimhe seo.

source: This new environment has different resources and different competitors, so the new population will need different features or adaptations to be a strong competitor than what they had needed before.
reference: Tá acmhainní difriúla agus iomaitheoirí difriúla sa timpeallacht nua seo, mar sin beidh gnéithe nó oiriúnuithe difriúla ag teastáil ón bpobal nua le dul san iomaíocht go láidir.

source: The original population hasn't changed at all, they still need the same adaptations as before.
reference: Tá an pobal bunaidh díreach mar a bhí riamh, agus na hoiriúnaithe céanna de dhíth orthu.

source: Over time, as the new population begins to adapt to their new environment, they start to look less and less like the other population.
reference: Le himeacht ama, de réir mar a thosaíonn an pobal nua ag oiriúnú dá dtimpeallacht nua, tarlaíonn sé go laghdaítear na cosúlachtaí idir iad agus an pobal eile.

source: Eventually, after thousands or even millions of years, the two populations will look so different that they can't be called the same species.
reference: Faoi dheireadh, tar éis na mílte bliain, nó na milliúin fiú, beidh cuma chomh difriúil óna chéile ar an dá phobal nach bhféadfaidh iad a ainmniú mar an speiceas céanna.

source: We call this process speciation, which just means the formation of new species. Speciation is an unavoidable consequence and a very important part of evolution.
reference: Speiceasú a thugaimid ar an bpróiseas seo, rud a chiallaíonn go díreach speiceas nua a chruthú. Is iarmhairt dosheachanta í an speiceasú agus is cuid an-tábhachtach den éabhlóid í.

source: Plants make oxygen which humans breathe, and they take in carbon-dioxide which humans exhale (that is, breathe out).
reference: Déanann plandaí ocsaigin a análaíonn daoine, agus glacann siad isteach dé-ocsaíd charbóin a easanálaíonn daoine (is é sin, a análaíonn siad amach).

source: Plants make their food from the sun by photosynthesis. They also provide shade.
reference: Déanann plandaí a gcuid bia ón ngrian trí fhótaisintéis. Cuireann siad scáth ar fáil, chomh maith.

source: We make our houses from plants and make clothes from plants. Most foods that we eat are plants. Without plants, animals could not survive.
reference: Déanaimid ár dtithe as plandaí agus déanaimid éadaí as plandaí. Is plandaí iad formhór na mbianna a ithimid. Gan phlandaí, ní fhéadfadh ainmhithe maireachtáil.

source: Mosasaurus was the apex predator of its time, so it feared nothing, except other mosasaurs.
reference: Bhí Mosasaurus ar an bhuaic-chreachadóir lena linn agus dá bhrí sin ní raibh eagla roimh rud ar bith seachas mosasaur eile.

source: Its long jaws were studded with more than 70 razor-sharp teeth, along with an extra set in the roof of its mouth, meaning that there was no escape for anything that crossed its path.
reference: Bhí a ghiallaí fada stodáilte le breis is 70 fiacail géara chomh maith le cár fiacla eile ina charball, rud a d'fhág nach raibh aon éalú uaidh i ndán do rud ar bith a thrasnaigh é.

source: We don't know for sure, but it may have had a forked tongue. Its diet included turtles, large fish, other mosasaurs, and it may even have been a cannibal.
reference: Níl a fhios againn go cinnte, ach b’fhéidir go raibh teanga dhá bheann aici. Áiríodh ar a aiste bia turtair, éisc mhóra, mosasáir eile, agus b’fhéidir gur canablach a bhí ann, fiú.

source: It also attacked anything that entered the water; even a giant dinosaur such as T. rex would be no match for it.
reference: D'ionsaigh sé freisin aon rud a tháinig isteach san uisce; ní bheadh ​​fiú dineasáir ollmhór cosúil leis an T. rex i ndán dó.

source: While most of their food would be familiar to us, Romans did have their share of strange or unusual feast items, including wild boar, peacock, snails, and a type of rodent called a dormouse
reference: Cé go mbeadh cur amach againn ar fhormhór a gcuid bia, bhí a gcion féin de mhíreanna aisteacha nó neamhghnácha féasta ag na Rómhánaigh, lena n-áirítear torc fiáin, an phéacóg, seilidí, agus cineál creimire ar a dtugtar an luch chodlamáin.

source: Another difference was that while the poor people and the woman ate their food while sitting in chairs, the rich men liked to have banquets together where they would lounge on their sides while they ate their meals.
reference: Rud eile a bhí difriúil, d'itheadh na daoine bochta agus na mná a gcuid bia ina suí i gcathaoir, ach b'fhearr do na fir saibhre an nós féasta a bheith acu le chéile ina luíodh siad ar a gcliathán agus iad i mbun a mbéilí a ithe.

source: Ancient Roman meals couldn't have included foods that came to Europe from America or from Asia in later centuries.
reference: Ní féidir gur ith muintir na Sean-Róimhe bianna a tugadh chun na hEorpa ó Mheiriceá nó ón Áise sna céadta ina dhiaidh sin.

source: For instance, they didn't have corn, nor tomatoes, nor potatoes, nor cocoa, and no ancient Roman ever tasted a turkey.
reference: Mar shampla, ní raibh arbhar, trátaí, prátaí ná cócó acu agus ní bhfuair sean-Rómhánach blas de thurcaí riamh.

source: The Babylonians built each of their gods a primary temple that was considered the home of the god.
reference: Thóg na Bablónaigh príomhtheampall do gach ceann dá ndéithe agus measadh gurb ansin a bhí cónaí ar an dia.

source: People would bring sacrifices to the gods and the priests would try to attend to the needs of the gods through ceremonies and festivals.
reference: Thabharfadh daoine íobairtí chuig na déithe agus dhéanfadh na sagairt iarracht freastal ar riachtanais na ndéithe trí shearmanais agus féilte.

source: Each temple had an open temple courtyard and then an inner sanctuary that only the priests could enter.
reference: I ngach teampall bhíodh clós teampaill ar oscailt agus ansin bhíodh tearmann rúin ann nach dtugtaí cead d'aon duine dul isteach ann ach sagairt.

source: Sometimes special pyramid shaped towers, called ziggurats, were built to be a part of the temples.
reference: Uaireanta tógadh túir speisialta ar chruth pirimide, ar a dtugtar siogúráit, le bheith mar chuid de na teampaill.

source: The top of the tower was special sanctuary for the god.
reference: Tearmann ar leith don dia a bhí i mbarr an túir.

source: In the warm climate of the Middle East, the house was not so important.
reference: In aeráid te an Mheánoirthir, ní raibh an teach chomh tábhachtach.

source: Most of the life of the Hebrew family happened in the open air.
reference: Tharla mórchuid de shaol clainne na nEabhrach amuigh faoin aer.

source: Women did the cooking in the yard; stores were just open counters looking into the street. Stone was used for building houses.
reference: Rinne mná an chócaireacht sa chlós; ní raibh sna siopaí ach cuntair oscailte ag féachaint isteach sa tsráid. As cloch a tógadh na tithe.

source: There were no large forests in the land of Canaan, so wood was extremely expensive.
reference: Ní raibh aon fhoraois mhór i dtalamh Chanán, a d'fhág go raibh adhmad an-chostasach.

source: Greenland was settled sparsely. In the Norse sagas they say that Erik the Red was exiled from Iceland for murder, and when travelling further west, found Greenland and named it Greenland.
reference: Níor lonnaíodh mórán daoine sa Ghraonlainn. Deirtear i ságaí na Lochlannach gur díbríodh Erik the Red ón Íoslainn mar gheall ar dhúnmharú, agus le linn dó taisteal níos faide siar, d'aimsigh sé an Ghraonlainn agus ba é an Ghraonlainn an t-ainm a thug sé uirthi.

source: But regardless of his discovery, Eskimo tribes were already living there at the time.
reference: Ach beag beann ar a raibh aimsithe aige, bhíodh treibheanna Eiscimigh ina gcónaí ann cheana ag an am sin.

source: Though each country was 'Scandinavian', there were many differences between the people, kings, customs and history of Denmark, Sweden, Norway and Iceland.
reference: Cé gur ‘tír Lochlannach’ ab ea gach tír acu, bhí go leor difríochtaí idir muintir, ríthe, nósanna agus stair na Danmhairge, na Sualainne, na hIorua agus na hÍoslainne.

source: If you have watched the movie National Treasure, you may think a treasure map was written on the back of the Declaration of Independence.
reference: Má bhreathnaigh tú ar an scannán National Treasure, b’fhéidir go gceapfá gur scríobhadh mapa taisce ar chúl an Dearbhú Neamhspleáchais.

source: However, that is not true. Although there is something written on the back of the document, it is not a treasure map.
reference: Níl sé seo fíor, áfach. Cé go bhfuil rud éigin scríofa ar chúl an doiciméid, ní mapa taisce é.

source: Written on the back of the Declaration of Independence were the words "Original Declaration of Independence dated 4th July 1776". The text appears on the bottom of the document, upside down.
reference: Scríofa ar chúl Fhorógra Saoirse Mheiriceá bhí na focail "Original Declaration of Independence dated 4th July 1776". Tá an téacs le feiceáil ag bun an doiciméid, bunoscionn.

source: While no one knows for certain who wrote it, it is known that early in its life, the large parchment document (it measures 29¾ inches by 24½ inches) was rolled up for storage.
reference: Cé nach fios go cinnte cé a scríobh é, tá a fhios againn go ndearnadh an doiciméad páir mhór (atá 29¾ orlach faoi 24½ orlach) a rolladh suas lena stóráil go luath ina shaol.

source: So, it is likely that the notation was added simply as a label.
reference: Mar sin, is dócha gur mar lipéad amháin a cuireadh an nodaireacht leis.

source: The D-Day landings and the following battles had freed the north of France, but the south still wasn't free.
reference: D'éirigh le Tuirlingtí D-Day agus na cathanna a lean é tuaisceart na Fraince a shaoradh, ach ní raibh an deisceart saor fós.

source: It was ruled by the "Vichy" French. These were French people who had made peace with the Germans in 1940 and worked with the invaders instead of fighting them.
reference: Bhí sé faoi smacht na Francaigh "Vichy". Ba mhuintir na Fraince iad seo rinne síocháin leis na Gearmánaigh i 1940 agus d'oibrigh leis na hionróirí in ionad troid leo.

source: On 15 August 1940, the Allies invaded southern France, the invasion was called "Operation Dragoon".
reference: Ar an 15 Lúnasa 1940, rinne na Comhghuaillithe ionradh ar dheisceart na Fraince, ionradh ar tugadh "Oibríocht Dragúin" air.

source: In just two weeks the Americans and Free French forces had liberated southern France and were turning towards Germany.
reference: Faoi cheann coicíse bhí na Meiriceánaigh agus fórsaí Saoire na Fraince tar éis deisceart na Fraince a shaoradh agus bhí siad ag casadh i dtreo na Gearmáine.

source: A civilization is a singular culture shared by a significant large group of people who live and work co-operatively, a society.
reference: Cultúr uathúil is ea sibhialtacht ina mbíonn grúpa mór suntasach daoine páirteach agus a mhaireann agus a oibríonn i gcomhar a chéile, sochaí atá ann.

source: The word civilization comes from the Latin civilis, meaning civil, related to the Latin civis, meaning citizen, and civitas, meaning city or city-state, and that also somehow defines the size of the society.
reference: Ón bhfocal Laidine civilis, a chiallaíonn sibhialta, a thagann an focal sibhialtacht; tá sé gaolmhar leis an bhfocal Laidin civis, a chiallaíonn saoránach agus civitas, a chiallaíonn cathair nó cathair-stáit, agus a shocraíonn méid a sochaí ar bhealach éigin, leis.

source: City-states are the precursors of nations. A civilizational culture implies the passing on of knowledge across several generations, a lingering cultural footprint and fair dissemination.
reference: Is réamhtheachtaithe náisiún iad cathairstáit. Tuigtear le cultúr sibhialtachta go mbíonn eolas á chur ar aghaidh thar roinnt glúnta, rian marthanach cultúrtha agus scaipeadh réasúnta i gceist leis.

source: Minor cultures often vanish without leaving relevant historic evidence and fail to be recognized as proper civilizations.
reference: Is minic a imíonn mionchultúir gan fianaise stairiúil ábhartha ar bith a fhágáil ina dhiaidh agus dá bharr sin ní aithnítear mar shibhialtachtaí cearta iad.

source: During the Revolutionary War, the thirteen states first formed a weak central government—with the Congress being its only component—under the Articles of Confederation.
reference: Le linn Chogadh na Réabhlóidithe, ar dtús chruthaigh na trí stát déag rialtas láir a bhí lag—agus an Chomhdháil an t-aon chuid a bhí ann—faoi Airteagail na Cónaidhme.

source: Congress lacked any power to impose taxes, and, because there was no national executive or judiciary, it relied on state authorities, who were often uncooperative, to enforce all its acts.
reference: Ní raibh sé de chumhacht ag an gComhdháil cánacha a fhorchur, agus, toisc nach raibh aon fheidhmeannach ná na breithiúna náisiúnta ann, bhí sí ag brath ar údaráis stáit lena hachtanna uile a chur i bhfeidhm, ach ba mhinic a dhiúltaigh na húdaráis sin don chomhoibriú.

source: It also had no authority to override tax laws and tariffs between states.
reference: Ní raibh aon údarás aige freisin dlíthe cánach ná taraifí idir stáit a shárú.

source: The Articles required unanimous consent from all the states before they could be amended and states took the central government so lightly that their representatives were often absent.
reference: B'éigean toiliú d'aon ghuth a fháil ó na stáit uile sula bhféadfaí na hAirteagail a leasú agus bhí na stáit chomh neamhdháiríre sin faoin rialtas lárnach gur minic a bhí a gcuid ionadaithe as láthair.

source: Italy's national football, along with German national football team is the second most successful team in the world and were the FIFA World Cup champions in 2006.
reference: Is í foireann peile náisiúnta na hIodáile, in éineacht le foireann peile náisiúnta na Gearmáine an dara foireann is rathúla ar domhan agus ba seaimpíní Chorn Domhanda FIFA iad i 2006.

source: Popular sports include football, basketball, volleyball, water-polo, fencing, rugby, cycling, ice hockey, roller hockey and F1 motor racing.
reference: Ar na spóirt is mó tóir tá peil, cispheil, eitpheil, póló uisce, pionsóireacht, rugbaí, rothaíocht, haca oighir, haca rollóra agus rásaíocht carr F1.

source: Winter sports are most popular in the Northern regions, with Italians competing in international games and Olympic events.
reference: Sna réigiúin ó thuaidh is mó atá tóir ar an spórt geimhridh, agus téann na hIodálaigh san iomaíocht i gcluichí idirnáisiúnta agus in imeachtaí Oilimpeacha.

source: Japans holds nearly 7,000 islands (the biggest being Honshu), making Japan the 7th largest island in the world!
reference: Tá thart ar 7,000 oileán sa tSeapáin (is é Honshu an ceann is mó), rud a fhágann gurbh í an tSeapáin an 7ú oileán is mó ar domhain!

source: Due to the cluster/group of islands Japan has, Japan is often referred to, on a geographical stance, as an "archipelago"
reference: Mar gheall ar an gcnuasach/grúpa oileán atá sa tSeapáin, is minic a ndéantar tagairt uirthi, i dtéarmaí tíreolaíocha, mar "oileánra"

source: Taiwan beginning start way back in 15th century where European sailors passing by record the island’s name as Ilha Formosa, or beautiful island.
reference: Chualathas trácht ar an Téaváin den chéad uair thiar sa 15ú haois nuair a bhaist mairnéalaigh Eorpacha a sheol thar an oileán Ilha Formosa nó oileán álainn ar an oileán.

source: In 1624,Dutch East India Company establishes a base in southwestern Taiwan, initiating a transformation in aboriginal grain production practices and employing Chinese laborers to work on its rice and sugar plantations.
reference: Bhunaigh Comhlacht Dúitseach na hIndia Thoir ceanncheathrú in iardheisceart Téaváin i 1624, rud a chuir tús le hathrú i gcleachtais táirgeachta maidir leis an ngrán dúchasach agus fostaíodh oibrithe ón tSín chun dul i mbun oibre ar na plandálacha ríse agus siúcra.

source: In 1683, Qing dynasty (1644-1912) forces take control of Taiwan’s western and northern coastal areas and declared Taiwan as a province of the Qing Empire in 1885.
reference: Sa bhliain 1683, ghlac fórsaí ríshliocht Qing (1644-1912) smacht ar cheantair chósta thiar agus thuaidh Téaváin agus dhearbhaigh siad Téaváin mar chúige d’Impireacht Qing i 1885.

source: In 1895, after defeat in the First Sino-Japanese War (1894-1895), the Qing government signs the Treaty of Shimonoseki, by which it cedes sovereignty over Taiwan to Japan, which rules the island until 1945.
reference: In 1895, tar éis gur chliseadh iad sa Chéad Chogadh Sino-Seapánach (1894-1895), síneann rialtas Qing Conradh Shimonoseki, ina ghéilleann siad ceannasaíocht thar Téaváin go dtí an tSeapáin, a rialaíonn an t-oileán go dtí 1945.

source: Machu Picchu consist of three main structures, namely Intihuatana, the Temple of the Sun, and the Room of the Three Windows.
reference: Tá Machu Picchu déanta suas de thrí phríomh-struchtúr, is iad sin Intihuatana, Teampall na Gréine agus Seomra na Trí Fhuinneoige.

source: Most of the buildings on the edges of the complex have been rebuilt in order to give tourists a better idea of how they originally appeared.
reference: Rinneadh cuid is mó de na foirgnimh ar imeall an choimpléisc a atógáil chun blaiseadh níos fearr den chuma a bhí orthu fadó a thabhairt do thurasóirí.

source: By 1976, thirty percent of Machu Picchu had been restored and restoration continues till today.
reference: Faoi 1976, rinneadh athchóiriú ar thríocha faoin gcéad de Machu Picchu and leanadh leis an athchóiriú go dtí an lá atá inniu againn.

source: For example, the most common still image photography format in the world is 35mm, which was the dominant film size at the close of the analog film era.
reference: Mar shampla, is í 35mm an fhormáid grianghrafadóireachta íomhá shocair is coitianta atá ar domhan, arb í sin an mhéid is mó úsáide sa scannánaíocht ag deireadh ré na scannán analógach.

source: It is still produced today, but more importantly its aspect ratio has been inherited by digital camera image sensor formats.
reference: Déantar é tháirgeadh sa lá ata inniu ann go fóill ach níos tábhachtaí arís tá a chóimheas treoíochta leanta ar aghaidh chuig formáidí braiteora íomhá ceamara digitigh.

source: The 35mm format is actually, somewhat confusingly, 36mm in width by 24mm in height.
reference: Tá an formáid 35 mm 36 mm ar leithead faoi 24 mm ar airde, i ndáiríre, rud a d'fhéadfadh mearbhall a chur ar dhuine.

source: The aspect ratio of this format (dividing by twelve to obtain the simplest whole-number ratio) is therefore said to be 3:2.
reference: Mar deirtear gurb ionann 3:2 agus cóimheas gné na formáide seo (roinnt ar a dó dhéag chun an cóimheas is simplí slánuimhreach a fháil).

source: Many common formats (APS family of formats, for example) are equal to or closely approximate this aspect ratio.
reference: Tá neart formáidí coitianta (formáidí APS mar shampla) cothrom le nó an-chóngarach don chóimheas treoíochta seo.

source: The much-abused and often-ridiculed rule of thirds is a simple guideline creating dynamism while keeping a measure of order in an image.
reference: Is treoirlíne shimplí í riail na dtréacha, cé go mbíonn daoine ag caitheamh anuas uirthi nó ag fonóid fúithi uaireanta, a chruthaíonn dinimiceas agus a chuireann ord éigin ar íomhá.

source: It states that the most effective place for the main subject is at the intersection of lines dividing the image into thirds vertically and horizontally (see example).
reference: Deir sé gurbh é an áit a dtrasnaíonn na línte a roinneann an íomhá ina thrí chuid go hingearach agus go cothrománach, an áit is éifeachtaí don phríomhábhar (féach sampla).

source: During this period of European history, the Catholic Church, which had become rich and powerful, came under scrutiny.
reference: Le linn na tréimhse sin i stair na hEorpa, tosaíodh ar scrúdú a dhéanamh ar an Eaglais Chaitliceach tar éis dí éirí an-saibhir agus cumhachtach.

source: For over a thousand years the Christian religion had bound European states together despite differences in language and customs. I
reference: Le breis is míle bliain anuas, ceanglaíodh stáit na hEorpa le chéile le creideamh na Críostaíochta in ainneoin teangacha agus gnásanna éagsúla.

source: Its all-pervading power affected everyone from king to commoner.
reference: Bhí tionchar ag a chumhacht forleitheadach ar gach duine ó ríthe go daoir.

source: One of the main Christian tenets is that wealth should be used to alleviate suffering and poverty and that the monetary funds of the church are there specifically for that reason.
reference: Is ceann de phríomhphrionsabail na Críostaíochta é gur cheart saibhreas a úsáid chun fulaingt agus bochtaineacht a mhaolú agus go bhfuil cistí airgeadaíochta na heaglaise ann go speisialta don chúis sin.

source: The central authority of the church had been in Rome for over a thousand years and this concentration of power and money led many to question whether this tenet was being met.
reference: Bhí údarás lárnach na heaglaise suite sa Róimh le breis agus míle bliain agus thug an comhchruinniú cumhachta agus airgid seo ar go leor daoine ceisteanna a chur faoi cibé an raibh an deargphrionsabal seo á chomhlíonadh.

source: Soon after the outbreak of hostilities, Britain initiated a naval blockade of Germany.
reference: Go luath tar éis bhriseadh amach na cogaíochta, chuir an Bhreatain an Ghearmáin faoi imshuí cabhlaigh.

source: The strategy proved effective, cutting off vital military and civilian supplies, although this blockade violated generally accepted international law codified by several international agreements of the past two centuries.
reference: Straitéis éifeachtach a bhí ann, trínar gearradh soláthairtí ríthábhachtacha míleata agus sibhialtach, cé gur sháraigh na smachtbhannaí seo an dlí idirnáisiúnta a nglactar leis go ginearálta agus atá códaithe i gcomhaontuithe idirnáisiúnta éagsúla le dhá chéad bliain anuas.

source: Britain mined international waters to prevent any ships from entering entire sections of ocean, causing danger to even neutral ships.
reference: Chuir an Bhreatain mianaigh in uiscí idirnáisúnta chun cosc a chur ar aon longa dul isteach i gcodanna iomlána d'aigéan, rud a chuir longa neodracha i mbaol fiú amháin.

source: Since there was limited response to this tactic, Germany expected a similar response to its unrestricted submarine warfare.
reference: Ó tharla freagairt theoranta ar an teaictic seo, bhí an Ghearmáin ag súil le freagairt den sórt céanna ina chogaíocht neamhtheoranta fomhuireáin.

source: During the 1920s, the prevailing attitudes of most citizens and nations was that of pacifism and isolation.
reference: I rith na 1920idí, ba iad an síochánachas agus an leithlisiú na meonta ab fhorleithne a bhí ann i measc formhór na saoránach agus na náisiún.

source: After seeing the horrors and atrocities of war during World War I, nations desired to avoid such a situation again in the future.
reference: Agus uafáis agus ainghníomhartha an chogaidh feicthe acu le linn an Chéad Chogadh Domhanda, ba mhian le náisiúin a leithéid de rud a sheachaint do na glúine le teacht.

source: In 1884, Tesla moved to the United States of America to accept a job with the Edison Company in New York City.
reference: In 1884, bhog Tesla go dtí na Stáit Aontaithe Mheiriceá chun post a ghlacadh leis an Edison Company i gCathair Nua Eabhrac.

source: He arrived in the US with 4 cents to his name, a book of poetry, and a letter of recommendation from Charles Batchelor (his manager in his previous job) to Thomas Edison.
reference: Nuair a shroich sé na Stáit Aontaithe ní raibh ina sheilbh ach 4 cent ina phóca, leabhar filíochta, agus litir mholta ó Charles Batchelor (an bainisteoir a bhí aige ina phost roimhe) chuig Thomas Edison.

source: Ancient China had a unique way of showing different time periods; each stage of China or each family that was in power was a distinctive dynasty.
reference: Sa sean-tSín d'úsáidtí bealach ar leith le tréimhsí ama difriúla a léiriú; gach céim den tSín nó gach clann a bhí i gceannas, ba ríshliocht sainiúil é.

source: Also between each dynasty was an unstable age of divided provinces. The best-known of these periods was the Three Kingdoms epoch taking place for 60 years between the Han and the Jin Dynasty.
reference: Chomh maith leis sin, idir gach ríshliocht tharlódh ré mhíshocair ina mbíodh na cúigí scoilte. An tréimhse is aitheanta díobh sin ab ea tréimhse na dTrí Ríocht a mhair 60 bliain idir na Ríshleachta Han agus Jin.

source: During these periods fierce warfare took place between many nobles fighting for the throne.
reference: Le linn na dtréimhsí sin bhíodh cogaíocht fhíochmhar idir go leor den uaisleacht a bhí ag troid chun seilbh a ghlacadh ar an ríchathaoir.

source: The Three Kingdoms was one of the bloodiest eras in Ancient China’s history thousands of people died fighting to sit in the highest seat in the grand palace at Xi’an.
reference: Ceann de na réanna ab fhuiltí i stair na Síne Ársa ab ea ré na dTrí Ríocht, nuair a fuair na mílte duine bás ag troid chun suí sa suíochán is airde sa mhórphálás ag Xi’an.

source: There are a lot of social and political effects such as the use of metric system, a shift from absolutism to republicanism, nationalism and the belief the country belongs to the people not to one sole ruler.
reference: Tá go leor éifeachtaí sóisialta agus polaitiúla ann mar shampla úsáid an chórais mhéadraigh, aistriú ón absalóideachas go dtí an poblachtachas, an náisiúnachas agus an creideamh go mbaineann an tír leis na daoine agus ní le rialóir aonair amháin.

source: Also after the Revolution occupations were open to all male applicants allowing the most ambitious and successful to succeed.
reference: Chomh maith leis sin, tar éis na Réabhlóide tugadh an deis gairme do gach iarratasóir fireann, rud a lig dóibh siúd a raibh an uaillmhian is airde acu agus an rath is mó orthu, éirí go maith lena shaol.

source: Same goes for the military because instead of army rankings being based on class they were now based on cailaber.
reference: Is ionann i gcás na míleatach de bhrí go bhfuil ranguithe airm bunaithe anois ar mhianach seachas ar aicme shóisialta.

source: The French Revolution also inspired many other repressed working class people of other country's to began their own revolutions.
reference: Spreag Réabhlóid na Fraince mórchuid den lucht saothair a bhí brúite faoi chois i dtíortha eile chun tús a chur lena réabhlóidí féin.

source: Muhammad was deeply interested in matters beyond this mundane life. He used to frequent a cave that became known as “Hira‘” on the Mountain of “Noor” (light) for contemplation.
reference: Chuir Muhammad spéis mhór i gcúrsaí spioradálta. Ba ghnách leis a mhachnamh a dhéanamh in uaimh ar a tugadh “Hira‘ ”ar Shliabh “Noor” (solas).

source: he cave itself, which survived the times, gives a very vivid image of Muhammad’s spiritual inclinations.
reference: Leis an bpluais féin, a maireann fós, tugtar íomhá an-bhríomhar do mhianta spioradálta Muhammad.

source: Resting on the top of one of the mountains north of Mecca, the cave is completely isolated from the rest of the world.
reference: Tá an phluais suite ar bharr ceann de na sléibhte ó thuaidh ó Mecca, agus tá sé scoite amach go hiomlán ón gcuid eile den domhan.

source: In fact, it is not easy to find at all even if one knew it existed. Once inside the cave, it is a total isolation.
reference: I ndáiríre tá sé deacair a fháil amach an raibh a fhios ag duine ar bith gurbh ann dó. Nuair atá tú istigh sa phluais, is leithlisiú iomlán é.

source: Nothing can be seen other than the clear, beautiful sky above and the many surrounding mountains. Very little of this world can be seen or heard from inside the cave.
reference: Ní féidir aon rud a fheiceáil seachas an spéir ghlan álainn thuas agus an iliomad sléibhte máguaird. Is beag den saol seo atá le feiceáil nó le cloisteáil ó laistigh den uaimh.

source: The Great Pyramid at Giza is the only one of the seven wonders that is still standing today.
reference: Ceann de na seachtar iontas atá fós ina seasamh inniu is ea an Phirimid Mhór ag Giza.

source: Built by the Egyptians in the third century BCE, the Great Pyramid is one of many large pyramid structures built to honor dead Pharaoh.
reference: Agus í tógtha ag na hÉigiptigh sa tríú haois RCR, is ceann amháin í an Phirimid Mór as go leor struchtúr móra pirimide ar tógadh le hómós a thabhairt do Farónna mairbhe.

source: The Giza Plateau, or "Giza Necropolis" in the Egyptian Valley of the Dead contains several pyramids (of which the great pyramid is the largest), several small tombs, several temples, and the great Sphinx.
reference: Tá roinnt pirimidí ar Ardchlár Giza, nó "Giza Necropolis" i nGleann na Rithe san Éigipt (arb í an phirimid mhór an ceann is mó), mar aon le roinnt tuamaí beaga, roinnt teampall, agus an Sfioncs Mór.

source: The great pyramid was created to honor the Pharaoh Khufu, and many of the smaller pyramids, tombs, and temples were built to honor Khufu's wives and family members.
reference: Tógadh an phirimid mhór in onóir Fharó Khufu, agus tógadh móchuid pirimidí beaga, tuamaí agus teampaill in onóir mná agus baill teaghlaigh de chuid Khufu.

source: The "up bow" mark looks like a V and the "down bow mark" like a staple or a square missing its bottom side.
reference: Tá an marc "bogha suas" an-chosúil le V agus tá an marc "bogha síos" cosúil le stápla nó le cearnóg a bhfuil a taobh íochtair ar iarraidh.

source: Up means you should start at the tip and push the bow, and down means you should start at the frog (which is where your hand is holding the bow) and pull the bow.
reference: Ciallaíonn suas gur chóir duit tosú ag an rinn agus an bogha a bhrú, agus ciallaíonn síos gur chóir duit tosú ag an bhfrog (sin an áit a bhfuil greim ag do lámh ar an mbogha) agus an bogha a tharraingt.

source: An up-bow usually generates a softer sound, while a down-bow is stronger and more assertive.
reference: Is iondúil a bhíonn fuaim níos séimhe le bogha suas, agus fuaim níos láidre agus ceannasaí le bogha síos.

source: Feel free to pencil in your own marks, but remember the printed bowing marks are there for a musical reason, so they should usually be respected.
reference: Níl fadhb ar bith le do chuid marcanna féin a bhreacadh síos, ach ná dearmad go mbíonn na marcanna boghta priontáilte ann ar chúis ceoil, mar sin ba chóir aird a thabhairt orthu dá bharr.

source: The terrified King Louis XVI, Queen Marie Antoinette their two young children (11 year old Marie Therese and four year old Louis-Charles) and the King's sister, Madam Elizabeth, on the 6th October 1789 were forced back to Paris from Versailles by a mob of market women.
reference: Thug drong ban margaidh ar an Rí Louis XVI, an Bhanríon Marie Antoinette, a mbeirt leanaí óga (Marie Therese 11 bhliain d’aois agus Louis-Charles ceithre bliana d’aois) agus deirfiúr an Rí, Madam Elizabeth, iad uile sceimhlithe, filleadh ar Pháras ó Versailles ar an 6 Deireadh Fómhair 1789.

source: In a carriage, they traveled back to Paris surrounded by a mob of people screaming and shouting threats against the King and Queen.
reference: I gcarráiste, thaistil siad ar ais go dtí Páras agus slua daoine timpeall orthu a bhí bagairtí á screadaíl agus á béicíl acu i gcoinne an Rí agus na Banríona.

source: The mob of people forced the King And Queen to have their carriage windows wide open.
reference: Chuir an drong daoine iachall ar an Rí agus ar an mBanríon fuinneoga a gcarráiste a choimeád lánoscailte.

source: At one point a member of the mob waved the head of a royal guard killed at Versailles in front of the terrified Queen.
reference: Ag pointe amháin chroith duine den tslua cloigeann de gharda ríoga, ar maraíodh ag Versailles é, díreach os comhair na Banríona scanraithe.

source: The war expenditures of U.S. imperialism in the conquest of the Philippines were paid for by the Filipino people themselves.
reference: Ba iad muintir na nOileán Filipíneach féin a d'íoc as na costais chogaidh a ghabh le himpiriúlachas na S.A. agus iad ag iarraidh na hOileáin Fhilipíneacha a chur faoi chois.

source: They were compelled to pay taxes to the U.S. colonial regime to defray a major part of the expenditures and the interest on bonds floated in the name of the Philippine government through the Wall Street banking houses.
reference: B'éigean dóibh cánacha a íoc le réimeas coilíneach na S.A. chun íoc as cuid mhór den chaiteachas agus den ús ar bhannaí a bunaíodh in ainm rialtas na nOileán Filipíneach trí thithe baincéireachta Wall Street.

source: Of course, the superprofits derived from the protracted exploitation of the Filipino people would constitute the basic gains of U.S. imperialism.
reference: Ar ndóigh, bheadh ​​na sárbhrabúis a fuarthas ón dúshaothrú tarraingthe amach a rinneadh ar mhuintir na nOileán Filipíneach ina mbunghnóthachain d'impiriúlachas na S.A.

source: To understand the Templars one must understand the context that prompted the creation of the order.
reference: Chun tuiscint a fháil ar na Teamplóirí, ní mór tuiscint a fháil ar an gcomhthéacs as ar fáisceadh an t-ord.

source: The age where the events took place is commonly referred as the High Middle Ages the period of European history in the 11th, 12th, and 13th centuries (AD 1000–1300).
reference: An Mheánaois Ard an t-ainm coitianta ar an tréimhse sin de stair na hEorpa sna 11ú, 12ú agus 13ú haoiseanna (AD 1000-1300) nuair a tharla na himeachtaí i dtrácht.

source: The High Middle Ages were preceded by the Early Middle Ages and followed by the Late Middle Ages, which by convention ends around 1500.
reference: Lean an Mheánaois Ard an Mheánaois Luath agus ina dhaidh sin tháinig an Mheánaois Dhéanach, a mheastar a tháinig chun deiridh timpeall 1500.

source: Technological determinism is a term that encompasses a wide range of ideas in practice, from technology-push or the technological imperative to a strict sense that human destiny is driven by an underlying logic associated with scientific laws and their manifestation in technology.
reference: Is téarma é cinnteacht theicneolaíoch a chuimsíonn raon leathan smaointe i bhfírinne, ó bhrú na teicneolaíochta nó an riachtanas teicneolaíochta go tuiscint dhian go bhfuil cinniúint an duine á tiomáint ag loighic bhunúsach a bhaineann le dlíthe eolaíochta agus a léiriú sa teicneolaíocht.

source: Most interpretations of technological determinism share two general ideas: that the development of technology itself follows a path largely beyond cultural or political influence, and that technology in turn has "effects" on societies that are inherent, rather than socially conditioned.
reference: Roinneann formhór léamha de chinnteachas teicneolaíochta dhá theoiric ghinearálta: go leanann forbairt na teicneolaíochta é féin bealach a sháraíonn tionchar cultúrtha nó polaitiúil agus go bhfuil "tionchair" ar shochaithe ag an teicneolaíocht dá réir sin atá ann ó dhúchas seachas foghlamtha go sóisialta.

source: For example, one might say that the motor car necessarily leads to the development of roads.
reference: Mar shampla, is féidir a rá toisc go mbíonn an carr ann, is gá go mbeidh forbairt na mbóithre ann.

source: However, a nationwide road network is not economically viable for just a handful of cars, so new methods of production are developed to reduce the cost of car ownership.
reference: Áfach, ní bhíonn gréasáin bóithre náisiúnta inmharthana do cúpla carr, mar sin forbraítear modhanna nua táirgiúlachta chun an costas a bhaineann le húinéireacht cairr a laghdú.

source: Mass car ownership also leads to a higher incidence of accidents on the roads, which leads to the invention of new techniques in healthcare for repairing damaged bodies.
reference: Bíonn níos mó timpistí ar na bóithre de bharr ollúinéireacht chairr agus as seo tagann cumadh de mhodhanna nua i gcúram sláinte chun coirp ghortuithe a chneasú.

source: Romanticism had a large element of cultural determinism, drawn from writers such as Goethe, Fichte, and Schlegel.
reference: Bhí gné mhór de chinnteacht chultúrtha ag gabháil leis an rómánsachas, a tógadh ó scríbhneoirí ar nós Goethe, Fichte, agus Schlegel.

source: In the context of Romanticism, the geography molded individuals, and over time customs and culture related to that geography arose, and these, being in harmony with the place of the society, were better than arbitrarily imposed laws.
reference: I gcomhthéacs an Rómánsachais, mhúnlaigh an tíreolaíocht daoine aonair, agus le himeacht aimsire d’eascair nósanna agus cultúr a bhain leis an tíreolaíocht sin, agus b’ iad seo, a bhí ar aon dul le háit na sochaí, níos fearr ná na dlíthe a chuireadh i bhfeidhm go héagórach.

source: In the manner that Paris is known as the fashion capital of the contemporary world, Constantinople was regarded as the fashion capital of feudal Europe.
reference: Sa chaoi is go dtugadh Páras ar phríomhchathair faisin an domhain chomhaimseartha, measadh gurb é Constantinople an príomhchathair faisin na hEorpa feodach.

source: Its renown for being an epicenter of luxury began in about 400 A.D. and lasted up until about 1100 A.D.
reference: Bhí cáil air mar chroílár an tsómais ó 400 A.D. amach go dtí  thart ar 1100 A.D.

source: Its status declined during the twelfth century mainly due to the fact that Crusaders had returned bearing gifts such as silks and spices that were valued more than what Byzantine markets offered.
reference: Tháinig meath ar a stádas linn an dara haois déag go príomha de bharr na gcrosáidí a d'fhill le bronntanais ar nós síodaí agus spíosraí a raibh níos luachmhaire ná an méid a bhí ar fáil sna margaí Biosántacha.

source: It was at this time that the transfer of the title of Fashion Capital from Constantinople to Paris was made.
reference: Bhí sé ag an am a rinneadh teideal de phríomhchathair an fhaisin a athrú ó Chathair Chonstaintín go Páras.

source: Gothic style peaked in the period between the 10th - 11th centuries and the 14th century.
reference: Ba é buaicphointe na stíle Gotaí sa tréimhse idir an 10ú - 11ú haois agus an 14ú haois.

source: At the beginning dress was heavily influenced by the Byzantine culture in the east.
reference: Sna laethanta tosaigh chuaigh cultúr na Biosáinte san oirthear go mór i bhfeidhm ar stíl éadaí.

source: However, due to the slow communication channels, styles in the west could lag behind by 25 to 30 year.
reference: Mar sin féin, mar gheall ar bhealaí cumarsáide a bheith mall, d'fhéadfadh moill de 25 nó 30 bliain a bheith ar stíleanna san oirthear.

source: towards the end of the Middle Ages western Europe began to develop their own style. one of the biggest developments of the time as a result of the crusades people began to use buttons to fasten clothing.
reference: i dtreo dheireadh na Meánaoise d'fhás a stíl féin i measc mhuintir iarthar na hEorpa, agus bhí sé seo ar cheann de na forbairtí ba mhó ag an am. Ba é a dtaithí ar na crosáidí a thug ar dhaoine cnaipí a úsáid le héadaigh a cheangal.

source: Subsistence agriculture is agriculture carried out for the production of enough food to meet just the needs of the agriculturalist and his/her family.
reference: Is éard is talmhaíocht chothabhála ann talmhaíocht trína dtáirgtear dóthain bia don saothróir agus dá theaghlach/teaghlach agus é sin amháin.

source: Subsistence agriculture is a simple, often organic, system using saved seed native to the ecoregion combined with crop rotation or other relatively simple techniques to maximize yield.
reference: Is córas simplí, orgánach é an talmhaíocht chothabhála go minic, ina n-úsáidtear síolta a sábháladh agus atá dúchasach don éiciréigiún sin i gcumasc le huainíocht barr nó teicnící réasúnta simplí eile chun an táirgeacht a uasmhéadú.

source: Historically most farmers were engaged in subsistence agriculture and this is still the case in many developing nations.
reference: Go stairiúil, bhí an chuid is mó d'fheirmeoirí i mbun na feirmeoireachta cothaitheacha agus is mar seo a bhíonn sé i bhformhór na dtíortha atá i mbéal forbartha.

source: Subcultures bring together like-minded individuals who feel neglected by societal standards and allow them to develop a sense of identity.
reference: Tugtar le chéile i bhfo-chultúir daoine a bhfuil an meon céanna acu agus a bhraitheann nach bhfuil slí dóibh sa ghnáthshochaí; is deis é dóibh tuiscint a fháil ar a bhféiniúlacht féin.

source: Subcultures can be distinctive because of the age, ethnicity, class, location, and/or gender of the members.
reference: Is féidir le fo-chultúir a bheith sainiúil de bharr aois, eitneacht, aicme, suíomh agus/nó inscne na mball.

source: The qualities that determine a subculture as distinct may be linguistic, aesthetic, religious, political, sexual, geographical, or a combination of factors.
reference: Is féidir gurb iad cáilíochtaí teangeolaíocha, aeistéitiúla, reiligiúnacha, polaitiúla, gnéasacha, geografacha, nó fachtóirí éagsúla i dteannta a chéile a chinneann cibé an fo-chultúr sainiúil é fo-chultúr ar leith.

source: Members of a subculture often signal their membership through a distinctive and symbolic use of style, which includes fashions, mannerisms, and argot.
reference: Is minic a thugann baill de fhochultúr a mballraíocht le fios trí shainstíl ar leith a úsáid go siombalach lena n-áirítear faisin, gothaíocht agus béarlagair.

source: One of the most common methods used to illustrate the importance of socialization is to draw upon the few unfortunate cases of children who were, through neglect, misfortune, or wilful abuse, not socialized by adults while they were growing up.
reference: Ceann de na modhanna is coitianta a úsáidtear chun tábhacht an tsóisialaithe a léiriú is ea tarraingt ar an gcúpla cás mífhortúnacha de leanaí nár sóisialaíodh le cabhair daoine fásta nuair a bhí siad ag fás, ar chúiseanna a bhain le faillí, mí-ádh nó mí-úsáid thoiliúil.

source: Such children are called "feral" or wild. Some feral children have been confined by people (usually their own parents); in some cases this child abandonment was due to the parents' rejection of a child's severe intellectual or physical impairment.
reference: Deirtear go bhfuil leanaí den sórt sin "fiáin". Tá leanaí fiáine áirithe ann a coimeádadh sáinnithe (uaireanta ag a dtuismitheoirí féin); i gcásanna áirithe ba é máchail trom intleachta nó fisiciúil ar an leanbh a thug air na tuismitheoirí an leanbh a thréigean.

source: Feral children may have experienced severe child abuse or trauma before being abandoned or running away.
reference: Is féidir go raibh mí-úsáid leanaí nó tráma tromchúiseach i gceist le leanaí fiáine sular thréigeadh iad nó sular éalaigh siad ón mbaile.

source: Others are alleged to have been brought up by animals; some are said to have lived in the wild on their own.
reference: Líomhnaítear gur ainmhithe a thóg daoine eile; deirtear go raibh cuid acu ina gcónaí san fhiántas leo féin.

source: When completely brought up by non-human animals, the feral child exhibits behaviors (within physical limits) almost entirely like those of the particular care-animal, such as its fear of or indifference to humans.
reference: Nuair a dhéanann ainmhithe neamh-dhaonna duine a thógáil ón tús, léiríonn an leanbh fiáin iompraíochtaí (laistigh de theorainneacha fisiciúla) atá beagnach mar an gcéanna le hiompar an ainmhí chúraim áirithe, mar shampla eagla roimh dhaoine nó neamhshuim iontu.

source: While project based learning should make learning easier and more interesting, scaffolding goes a step beyond.
reference: Cé gur cheart go bhfágfadh foghlaim bunaithe ar thionscadal go mbeadh an fhoghlaim níos fusa agus níos suimiúla, téann scafláil céim níos faide anonn.

source: Scaffolding is not a method of learning but rather an aid that provides support to individuals whom are undergoing a new learning experience such as using a new computer program or beginning a new project.
reference: Ní modh foghlama é an scafláil ach áis a thugann tacaíocht do dhaoine atá ag dul faoi eispéireas nua foghlama ar nós ríomhchlár nua a úsáid nó tionscadal nua a thosú.

source: Scaffolds can be both virtual and real, in other words, a teacher is a form of scaffold but so is the little paperclip man in Microsoft Office.
reference: Is féidir le scafláil a bheith fíorúil agus fíor, i bhfocail eile, is cineál scaflála é múinteoir ach is amhlaidh i gcás an fhir bhig fáiscín páipéir in Microsoft Office.

source: Virtual Scaffolds are internalized in the software and are meant to question, prompt, and explain procedures that may have been to challenging for the student to handle alone.
reference: Déantar Scafaill Fhíorúla a inmheánú sna bogearraí agus tá siad ceaptha le nósanna imeachta, a d’fhéadfadh a bheith dúshlánach don mhac léinn a láimhseáil leo féin, a cheistiú, a spreagadh agus a mhíniú.

source: Children are placed in Foster Care for a wide variety of reasons that range from neglect, to abuse, and even to extortion.
reference: Cuirtear leanaí faoi Chúram Altrama ar go leor cúiseanna difriúla a chlúdaíonn idir faillí a dhéanamh iontu, mí-úsáid orthu agus sracaireacht fiú amháin.

source: No child should ever have to grow up in an environment that is not nurturing, caring, and educational, but they do.
reference: Níor chóir go mbeadh ar aon leanbh riamh fás i dtimpeallacht nach bhfuil cothaitheach, comhbhách agus oideachasúil, ach is amhlaidh atá.

source: We perceive the Foster Care System to be a safety zone for these children.
reference: Braithimid gur crios sábháilteachta do na leanaí seo an Córas Cúraim Altrama.

source: Our foster care system is supposed to provide safe homes, loving caregivers, stable education, and reliable health care.
reference: Ceapadh ár gcóras cúraim altrama le tithe sábháilte, cúramóirí grámhara, oideachas cobhsaí, agus cúram sláinte iontaofa a sholáthar.

source: Foster care is supposed to provide all the necessities that were lacking in the home they were previously taken from.
reference: Tá cúram altrama ceaptha leis na riachtanais uile a bhí in easnamh sa bhaile, ónar tógadh na leanaí roimhe sin, a chur ar fáil dóibh.

source: The Internet combines elements of both mass and interpersonal communication.
reference: Comhcheanglaíonn an tIdirlíon eilimintí de ollchumarsáid agus den chumarsáid idirphearsanta.

source: The distinct characteristics of the Internet lead to additional dimensions in terms of the uses and gratifications approach.
reference: Leanann toisí breise as saintréithe an Idirlín i dtéarmaí chur chuige na n-úsáidí agus na sásamh.

source: For example, “learning” and “socialization” are suggested as important motivations for Internet use (James et al., 1995).
reference: Mar shampla, deirtear gurb iad "foghlaim" agus "sóisialú" a spreagann mórchuid daoine leis an idirlíon a úsáid (James et al., 1995).

source: “Personal involvement” and “continuing relationships” were also identified as new motivation aspects by Eighmey and McCord (1998) when they investigated audience reactions to websites.
reference: D'aithin Eighmey agus McCord (1998) "rannpháirtíocht phearsanta" agus "caidreamh leanúnach" mar ghnéithe nua inspreagtha nuair a rinne siad imscrúdú ar fhreagairtí pobail i leith láithreáin ghréasáin.

source: The use of video recording has led to important discoveries in the interpretation of micro-expressions, facial movements which last a few milliseconds.
reference: De bharr úsáid na fístaifeadta, tá fionnachtana tábhachtacha déanta ó thaobh micreadhreacha, gothaí gnúise a mhaireann cúpla milleasoicind, a léirmhíniú.

source: In particular, it is claimed that one can detect whether a person is lying by interpreting micro-expressions correctly.
reference: Éilítear go háirithe gur féidir le duine a bhrath má tá an duine ag insint bréaga tríd bhrí a bhaint as mhicrea-nathanna.

source: Oliver Sacks, in his paper The President's Speech, indicated how people who are unable to understand speech because of brain damage are nevertheless able to assess sincerity accurately.
reference: Thug Oliver Sacks, ina pháipéar The President's Speech, le fios go bhfuil daoine nach bhfuil in ann urlabhra a thuiscint mar gheall ar dhamáiste inchinne in ann dáiríreacht a mheas go cruinn.

source: He even suggests that such abilities in interpreting human behavior may be shared by animals such as domestic dogs.
reference: Molann sé fiú go bhféadfaidh sé go bhfuil an cumas sin le ciall a bhaint as iompraíocht daoine ag ainmhithe amhail madraí tí.

source: Twentieth century research has shown that there are two pools of genetic variation: hidden and expressed.
reference: Tá sé léirithe ag taighde san fhichiú haois go bhfuil dhá linn d'éagsúlacht gheiniteach: folaithe agus léirithe.
